perturbation.original_text: Cargill agrees to perform the services to develop or modify CB1 to produce succinic acid and salts thereof using dextrose (defined as glucose) and/or sucrose as the fermentation feedstock ("Work Plan"). The Work Plan is more fully described in Exhibit A, which is hereby incorporated by reference into this Development Agreement. CB1 that has been further developed or modified (or the like) under the Work Plan shall be referred to as "Modified CB1". Any changes to the Work Plan must be in writing and signed by both Cargill and Bioamber and may be subject to incremental fees depending on resource requirements.
perturbation.changed_text: Cargill agrees to use its best efforts to perform the services to develop or modify CB1 to produce succinic acid and salts thereof using dextrose (defined as any sugar) and/or sucrose as the fermentation feedstock ("Work Plan"). The Work Plan is more fully described in Exhibit A, which is hereby incorporated by reference into this Development Agreement. CB1 that has been further developed or modified (or the like) under the Work Plan shall be referred to as "Modified CB1". Any changes to the Work Plan must be in writing and signed by both Cargill and Bioamber and may be subject to incremental fees depending on resource requirements.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "agrees to perform the services" to "agrees to use its best efforts to perform the services" weakens Cargill's obligation, making its commitment ambiguous and creates an in-text contradiction, since other sections assume a defined obligation. Also, by redefining 'dextrose' as 'any sugar' expands the scope of the Work Plan beyond what might be intended or defined elsewhere in the document. This contradicts the explicit mention of 'glucose' later and can create issues in the 'Alternative Feedstock' section, where specific milestones and payments are outlined for cellulosic biomass, creating confusion over what falls under the Work Plan and its associated fees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Bioamber also desires the right to further develop CB1 to utilize cellulosic biomass as the fermentation feedstock. Cargill hereby grants Bioamber the option to modify or convert the Work Plan to include the development or modification of CB1 capable of fermenting such cellulosic feedstock ("Modified Work Plan"). If such option is exercised, (a) the terms and conditions of this Development Agreement shall apply to the Modified Work Plan except (i) new, additional up-front and milestone payments shall apply as provided below, and (ii) any development work performed under the Modified Work Plan will be at a FTE rate which will be equivalent to the FTE rate provided in Section 2.2 above and further adjusted for inflation at the time it goes into effect; and (b) the commercial license attached as Exhibit D shall be expanded to include the resulting strain developed out of the Modified Work Plan with no additional change in the financial terms. Additionally, the additional up-front fee [***]. Milestone using Alternative Feedstock Target Date Payment Up-front Payment TBD US $[***] Milestone 1: Proof of Concept TBD US $[***] Milestone 2: CB1 Strain Development TBD US $[***] Milestone 3: CB1 Strain Optimization TBD US $[***]
perturbation.justification: The change from "agrees to perform the services" to "agrees to use its best efforts" weakens Cargill's obligation, introducing ambiguity regarding their commitment to the Work Plan. This contradicts the fixed milestone payments outlined in the 'Contradicted Text' relating to alternative feedstocks, as Cargill's 'best efforts' may not guarantee achievement of those milestones. Additionally, redefining 'dextrose' as 'any sugar' broadens the scope of the Work Plan, potentially impacting the 'Alternative Feedstock' section, where specific milestones and payments are outlined for cellulosic biomass. This expansion introduces ambiguity about which feedstocks fall under the original Work Plan versus the Modified Work Plan, creating a direct conflict in terms of applicable fees and milestones, satisfying the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees to disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party only to employees or agents of the Receiving Party who are directly involved with the Work Plan contemplated by this Development Agreement, and even then only to such extent as is necessary and essential to perform the Work Plan. The Receiving Party agrees to inform such employees and agents of the confidential nature of the information disclosed hereunder and to cause all such employees and agents to abide by the terms of this Development Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees to disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party only to employees, agents, or consultants of the Receiving Party who are involved with the general subject matter of this Development Agreement, and even then only to such extent as is deemed appropriate by the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party agrees to inform such individuals of the confidential nature of the information disclosed hereunder and to encourage all such individuals to abide by the general principles of this Development Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'directly involved with the Work Plan' to 'involved with the general subject matter' and 'necessary and essential' to 'deemed appropriate by the Receiving Party', the scope of who can receive confidential information broadens significantly and is up to the Receiving Party. This contradicts the strict confidentiality obligations detailed in 4.1 and 4.4 regarding the limited use and protection of Confidential Information, creating an in-text contradiction. Section 4.4 emphasizes that Confidential Information should only be used in connection with the Work Plan, not the 'general subject matter.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party will use Confidential Information only in connection with the Work Plan. Both Parties have reserved all rights to their respective Confidential Information not expressly granted herein. All documents and/or tangible materials containing or comprising Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party will remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will destroy all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and any documents prepared by the Receiving Party using Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party agrees to provide confirmation of such destruction in writing. The Receiving Party may, however, keep one copy of any such document in the files of its legal department or outside counsel for record purposes only.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly broadens the scope of who can access confidential information ('involved with the general subject matter' instead of 'directly involved with the Work Plan') and allows the Receiving Party to determine the extent of disclosure ('deemed appropriate by the Receiving Party' instead of 'necessary and essential'). This directly contradicts the original clause, which mandates confidential information be used 'only in connection with the Work Plan,' and the new change provides room for the Receiving party to make use of the data without necessarily involving or being a part of the work plan. This is a strong in-text contradiction because it directly conflicts with the strict confidentiality obligations in 4.1 and 4.4 and creates ambiguity, likely causing disputes about the intended scope of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: In the event Cargill does not achieve a given Milestone provided in Section 2.3 by the Target Date listed in Section 2.3 or modified Milestone and modified Target Date as agreed to by the Parties, and subsequently achieves such milestone as per the criteria described in Exhibit A, the payment for such missed milestone shall be due [***] and the amount due shall be [***] for every [***] beyond the Target Date. The Target Date for subsequent Milestones will be adjusted to reflect the date on which the Milestone was actually achieved. If a subsequent Milestone is achieved by the original Target Date listed in Section 2.3, Bioamber will pay Cargill the amount [***]. For purposes of clarity and as examples, if Milestones 1 and 2 were not delivered by the Target Dates, but Milestone 3 is delivered by or before the Target Date, then the total payments due to Cargill at that time would be [***] (a total payment of $[***]). Another example is if [***], the payment due at that time would be a total of $[***].
perturbation.changed_text: In the event Cargill experiences reasonable difficulties in achieving a given Milestone provided in Section 2.3 by the Target Date listed in Section 2.3 or modified Milestone and modified Target Date as agreed to by the Parties, and subsequently achieves such milestone based on a reasonable interpretation of the criteria described in Exhibit A, the payment for such milestone shall be subject to good faith negotiation between the parties. The Target Date for subsequent Milestones may be adjusted at Bioamber's discretion. If a subsequent Milestone is achieved near the original Target Date listed in Section 2.3, Bioamber may pay Cargill an amount deemed appropriate. For illustrative purposes, if Milestones 1 and 2 were significantly delayed, but Milestone 3 is delivered shortly after the Target Date, then the payments due to Cargill at that time will be discussed. Another example is if [***], the payment due at that time would be a total of $[***].
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'does not achieve' with 'experiences reasonable difficulties in achieving,' 'as per the criteria' with 'based on a reasonable interpretation of the criteria,' and setting payment for a missed Milestone subject to 'good faith negotiation', the obligation on Cargill to achieve milestones and the payment amounts become very ambiguous. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2.3, which originally specifies fixed payment amounts for achieving Milestones by certain Target Dates. The original section provides a clear framework, whereas the modified version introduces uncertainty and potential disagreement over milestone achievement and payment due, weakening the financial obligation of Bioamber.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Bioamber shall also pay Cargill within thirty (30) days of achieving each of the milestones summarized below and more fully described in the Work Plan found in Exhibit A. For purposes of clarity, payment is triggered [***]. Further, each of the Milestones and Target Dates may be changed according to the needs of the Work Plan and upon written agreement by the parties. Milestone Target Date Payment Milestone 1: Proof of Concept 12 months after Effective Date US $250,000.00 Milestone 2: CB1 Strain Development 30 months after Effective Date US $300,000.00 Milestone 3: CB1 Strain Optimization 42 months after Effective Date US $500,000.00
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity regarding milestone achievement and payment terms, replacing clear obligations with 'good faith negotiation' and discretionary payments. This directly contradicts the original section and the contradicted section, which specifies fixed payment amounts for achieving milestones by target dates. The modifications weaken Bioamber's financial obligations, creating a clear inconsistency in contract enforcement, therefore according to 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' a contradiction exists.
